LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. THE CITY ESTABLISHED THE ELEMENTS OF DESIGN IMMUNITY. (GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 830.6). DUSSEAULT DECLARATION AND HIS DEPOSITION TESTIMONY SHOW THAT HE REVIEWED THE PLAN AND ENSURED THAT IT COMPLIED WITH BOTH NATIONAL AND CITY STANDARDS REGARDING THE AMOUNT OF TIME ALLOCATED TO AN AVERAGE PEDESTRIAN CROSSING THE STREET. HE APPROVED THE 2003 SIGNAL PLAN THAT WAS IN EFFECT AT THE TIME OF THE ACCIDENT. THE FOLKS DECLARATION UNDERSCORED THE REASONABLENESS OF THE PLAN. SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE OF REASONABLENESS DEMONSTRATED. PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT ESTABLISHED A LOSS OF DESIGN IMMUNITY BY INADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE OF POST ACCIDENT COMPLAINTS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: MARIA VISCIO, CSR #12353

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco Motion For Summary Judgment (Continued From Oct-01-2009)
Off Calendar Oct-06-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Defendant City and County of San Francisco Motion for summary judgment. Appearances: Lesley Harris, Esq. Attorney at Law, (831) 458-0502 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Scott D. Wiener, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) 554-4283 appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary judgment granted. The City established the elements of design immunity. (Government Code section 830.6). Dusseault declaration and his deposition testimony show that he reviewed the plan and ensured that it complied with both national and city standards regarding the amount of time allocated to an average pedestrian crossing the street. He approved the 2003 signal plan that was in effect at the time of the accident. The Folks declaration underscored the reasonableness of the plan. Substantial evidence of reasonableness demonstrated. Plaintiffs have not established a loss of design immunity by inadmissible evidence of post accident complaints. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Maria Viscio, CSR #12353
